security_trigger_reason: F-0029 implements Telegram egress with operator-only recipient admission, no caller-supplied recipient, bot token handling, durable outbox text authority, terminal timeout behavior, no automatic resend of ambiguous sending rows, and fake Bot API smoke surfaces.

evidence: apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1156 rejects caller-supplied recipient fields through strict telegram.sendMessage parameter parsing before transport
apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1186 binds sends to an existing Telegram stimulus whose chat id matches the configured operator and :1216 refuses non-private contexts
apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1231 records durable outbox intent before transport and :1290 sends the stored outbox text on replay
apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1313 treats telegram_api_timeout as terminal failure and :1345-1356 passes an AbortSignal timeout to fetch
packages/db/src/telegram-egress.ts:295 claims only pending/retry_scheduled rows; :380 listReadyToRetry excludes ambiguous sending rows
packages/contracts/src/actions.ts:64 defines strict text-only telegram.sendMessage parameters with no recipient field
apps/core/src/platform/core-config.ts:207-224 fails closed when egress lacks token/operator chat or operator chat is not allowlisted
infra/docker/fake-telegram-api/server.py:93 implements fake sendMessage and infra/docker/compose.smoke-telegram.yaml:2 keeps it on the smoke overlay network

notes: PASS on current HEAD a45a9cdcd0a1. No high-confidence security findings in operator-only enforcement, no caller-supplied recipient, token leakage controls, durable outbox text authority, terminal timeout failure behavior, no automatic resend of ambiguous sending rows, or fake Bot API smoke surfaces. Review used the supplied verification artifact; no tests were rerun in this reviewer session.
